benchmark_results.query: What are the best museums in Lisbon?
benchmark_results.response:
**1.** 🏛️ **National Museum of Natural History and Science** - ⭐ 3.5/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Lisbon's main natural history museum covering collections in zoology, palaeontology and historic scientific instruments — good for families and science lovers.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Consult official website
- 💡 **Tip**: Check temporary exhibitions and guided-visit schedules on the website.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/national-museum-of-natural-history-and-science)**

**2.** 🏛️ **Museum of the Lisbon Geographical Society** - ⭐ 4.6/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Small museum dedicated to the history of Portuguese exploration and cartography — free entry and ideal for anyone interested in Age of Discovery maps and navigation instruments.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Consult official website
- 💡 **Tip**: Free admission makes it a good quick stop when touring Belém or central Lisbon.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/museum-of-the-lisbon-geographical-society)**

**3.** 🏛️ **National Museum of Sport** - ⭐ 2.9/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Interactive displays about Portugal’s sporting history, suitable for visitors interested in football and national sports culture.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Consult official website
- 💡 **Tip**: Family-friendly; check for temporary exhibits tied to major sporting events.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/national-museum-of-sport)**

**4.** 🏛️ **Aqueducts and Mãe d'Água (Águas Livres Aqueduct / Mãe d'Água Reservoir Museum)** - ⭐ 4.4/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Historic engineering site showing Lisbon’s 18th-century aqueduct and the atmospheric Mãe d'Água reservoir — part museum, part architectural landmark.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Today: Closed (check website for updates)
- 💡 **Tip**: Great for photography and to learn about Lisbon’s water supply history.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/aqueducts-and-mae-d-agua)**

**5.** 🏛️ **Water Museum - Barbadinhos Steam Pumping Station** - ⭐ 3.8/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Industrial heritage museum showcasing steam engines and the development of Lisbon’s water infrastructure.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Today: Closed (check website)
- 💡 **Tip**: Visit if you like industrial archaeology and historic machinery.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/water-museum-barbadinhos-steam-pumping-station)**

**6.** 🏛️ **Santa Marta Lighthouse Museum** - ⭐ 4.2/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Small museum inside an active lighthouse with exhibits on lighthouses and maritime signaling; offers a unique viewpoint over the city/river.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Consult official website
- 💡 **Tip**: Combine with a walk in the nearby area for panoramic views.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/santa-marta-lighthouse-museum)**

**7.** 🏛️ **Monument to the Discoveries** - ⭐ 4.2/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Monument celebrating the Age of Discovery with an exhibition inside about Portuguese explorers and a terrace with river views — technically a monument with museum elements.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Today: **10:00 - 19:00**
- 💡 **Tip**: Visit late afternoon for better light for photos of the Tagus.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/monument-to-the-discoveries)**

**8.** 🏛️ **Castle of the Moors (Castelo dos Mouros)** - ⭐ 4.5/5
- 📝 **Brief description**: Historic castle with defensive walls and viewpoints over Sintra and Lisbon; often listed with monuments rather than a traditional museum but includes interpretive displays.
- 📍 **Address**: Lisbon (exact address not provided in data)
- 🕒 **Opening hours**: Today: **09:00 - 18:30**
- 💡 **Tip**: Expect uphill walking; wear comfortable shoes.
- 🌐 **[Official page](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places/castle-of-the-moors)**

📌 **Source:** [*VisitLisboa*](https://www.visitlisboa.com/en/places)
expected_facts: List of top museums (e.g., Gulbenkian, MAAT, Museu Nacional do Azulejo)

benchmark_results.query: How do I get from Baixa-Chiado to Aeroporto?
benchmark_results.response:
🚇 **Baixa-Chiado → Aeroporto**

⏳ **Tempo total estimado:** ~35 min

🗺️ **O seu Trajeto de Metro:**
- 📍 **Embarque na estação Baixa-Chiado**
- 🟢 **Linha Verde** - direção **Telheiras**
- 🔄 **Transferência em Alameda**
- 🔴 **Linha Vermelha** - direção **Aeroporto**
- 🎯 **Saia na estação Aeroporto**
- 🚶 **Siga a pé para Aeroporto Humberto Delgado**

🗓️ **Próximos Metros** (tempo real):
- **Estação Baixa-Chiado:** Direção Telheiras — **⏱️ Próximo Metro em:** 3 min 28s | 11 min 54s
- **Estação Alameda:** Direção Aeroporto — **⏱️ Próximo Metro em:** Sem dados em tempo real

💡 **Dica rápida:** Tenha o cartão Viva Viagem carregado; o aeroporto é a última estação da linha Vermelha.

📌 **Fonte:** [*Metro de Lisboa*](https://www.metrolisboa.pt) | **Atualizado:** 21:19
expected_facts: Start at Baixa-Chiado (Azul/Verde lines)
Take Verde line to Alameda, transfer to Vermelha line
Ride Vermelha line to Aeroporto (terminal station)
Alternative: Azul line to São Sebastião, transfer to Vermelha

benchmark_results.query: What are the best museums in Lisbon?
benchmark_results.response:
Here are some of the best museums to visit in Lisbon, spanning art, history, and culture:

**1. MAAT – Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology**
- Brief description: Contemporary art and architecture with striking riverside building and rotating exhibitions.
- Morada: Avenida Brasília, 130, 1300-598 Lisboa
- Hours: Check website for current hours
- Tip: Great riverside walk and modern architecture combine well with a visit to the nearby Belém area.

**2. Museo Nacional de Arte Antiga (National Gallery of Ancient Art)**
- Brief description: Portugal’s premier collection of European painting from the 13th to the 19th centuries, plus important Portuguese works.
- Morada: Rua do Aleixo, 4, 1900-060 Lisboa (Note: verify exact address in data)
- Hours: Check official site for hours
- Tip: Pair with nearby Jerónimos Monastery in Belém for a full historical day.

**3. Berardo Collection Museum (Museu Coleção Berardo)**
- Brief description: Modern and contemporary art featuring Picasso, Warhol, Duchamp, and more in a dynamic space.
- Morada: Centro Cultural de Belém, Mosteiro dos Jerónimos, Praça do Império, 1400-206 Lisboa
- Hours: Check website for hours
- Tip: Located in Belém; combine with Pastéis de Belém and the riverside promenade.

**4. National Museum of Ancient Art – Parking**
- Brief description: (If you’re encountering this one as a duplicate, the main National Gallery of Ancient Art is the key stop for classical European works.)
- Morada: (As above)
- Hours: Check site

**5. Dr. Anastacio (Gulbenkian) Museum – Calouste Gulbenkian Museum**
- Brief description: World-class collection spanning ancient to 20th-century art, plus beautiful gardens.
- Morada: Avenida de Berna, 45A, 1067-001 Lisboa
- Hours: Check official site
- Tip: The garden is worth a stroll even on cooler days.

**6. National Museum of Contemporary Art (Museu Nacional de Arte Contemporânea – Chiado)**
- Brief description: Portuguese art from the 20th century to today; rotating exhibitions.
- Morada: Rua Serpa Pinto, 9, 1200-434 Lisboa
- Hours: Check official site
- Tip: Located in the Chiado area, good for combining with cafés and shops.

If you’d like, I can tailor a short museum day plan depending on your interests (classical European art, modern/contemporary, Portuguese masters) and provide exact current hours and entry prices. I can also pull up a map route including opening times for today.

📌 **Fonte:** *VisitLisboa* (Lisbon museums and cultural sites)
